front: Poem: contains "Constantly Risking Absurdity," "I Am Waiting," and "Junkman's Obbligato"
back: A Coney Island of the Mind (Lawrence Ferlinghetti)

front: Poem: speaker waits for "a new rebirth of wonder" and "retribution for what America did to Tom Sawyer"
Poem: envisions the "fleeing lovers on the Grecian Urn" finally catching each other
Poem: describes a "real live barking democratic dog" with "something to say about ontology" that looks "like a living questionmark"
Poem: compares a poet to an acrobat climbing "on rime / to a high wire of his own making"
Poem: declares "the world is the best place of all" but "right in the middle of it comes the smiling mortician"
back: The World is a Beautiful Place (Lawrence Ferlinghetti)
work: The World is a Beautiful Place

front: Author: published Ginsberg's "Howl" and was prosecuted on obscenity charges
Author: witnessed the aftermath of the bombing of Nagasaki while in the Navy
Author: "Populist Manifesto" asks "where are Whitman's wild children"
Author: poem "Underwear" riffs on Dylan Thomas with "Do not go naked into that good night"
